heading: Personal, living, and family expenses
label: 262 Personal, living, and family expenses
notes: Amendments1988— amended section generally. Prior to amendment, section read as follows: “Except as otherwise expressly provided in this chapter, no deduction shall be allowed for personal, living, or family expenses.”Pub. L. 100–647

Effective Date of 1988 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 100–647, title V, § 5073(b)102 Stat. 3682Nov. 10, 1988“The amendment made by subsection (a) [amending this section] shall apply to taxable years beginning after .”December 31, 1988
amendments: Amendments1988— amended section generally. Prior to amendment, section read as follows: “Except as otherwise expressly provided in this chapter, no deduction shall be allowed for personal, living, or family expenses.”Pub. L. 100–647

children.heading: General rule
children.label: a General rule
children.text: Except as otherwise expressly provided in this chapter, no deduction shall be allowed for personal, living, or family expenses.

children.heading: Treatment of certain phone expenses
children.label: b Treatment of certain phone expenses
children.text: For purposes of subsection (a), in the case of an individual, any charge (including taxes thereon) for basic local telephone service with respect to the 1st telephone line provided to any residence of the taxpayer shall be treated as a personal expense.
